codeReview.summary: The commit stays within the readiness-surface scope: it reconciles `/health` with `/ping`, adds machine-readable readiness booleans to runtime diagnostics, and tightens readiness-focused tests for live, degraded, validation-override, and zero-live boot states. I did not find a correctness issue in the shipped diff relative to the feature brief.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker hit an `EADDRINUSE` condition on port 3102 during manual verification because a prior validation API process was already listening there; reviewer-facing guidance says to stop any existing 3102 listener first, but `.factory/services.yaml` only defines start/stop commands and does not document that this collision is a known recurring validator hazard.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker transcript 68435f74-8b1f-412a-9251-fe4d530c66bc shows the first 3102 start collided and the handoff records a non-blocking issue about an existing process on 3102; compare AGENTS.md Testing & Validation Guidance ('Before starting the isolated validation API on 3102, stop any existing listener on that port').

summary: Reviewed commit 603cec05ece3933de586479600a13db62592834a for `baseline-readiness-surface-hardening`. The implementation satisfies the assigned readiness-surface scope and passes review; the only noted follow-up is a shared-state/services observation about recurring 3102 port-collision handling during validation runs.
